headline: ‘Freak out’ indicator soars to record with war sparking trader anxiety
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Investors wary of being wrong-footed by twists and turns in the Iran war are trading stocks at a record pace, one market measure shows. With investors hanging on every development in the Middle East and the often unpredictable missives of President Donald Trump, gauges of trading volume have spiked. One such metric, the daily turnover in the State Street SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust, has breached $60 billion — a level seen as a “freak out” indicator by Bloomberg Intelligence strategists

headline: BlackRock, State Street File for First ETFs to Challenge Invesco’s QQQ
summary: Invesco's Nasdaq 100-tracking QQQ exchange-traded fund is finally getting some competition. BlackRock filed paperwork with regulators for a rival ETF tracking the tech-heavy stock index on Monday, and State Street followed suit a day later.
